---
category: productivity
title: Pptx
name: pptx
description: "Use when creating, reading, editing, or manipulating PowerPoint presentations (.pptx) — slides, templates, speaker notes, animations."
---

## Description

Create, read, edit, and manipulate PowerPoint presentations (.pptx files). Includes creating slide decks, reading/extracting text, editing presentations, combining/splitting files, and working with templates.

## When to Use

- Creating or editing PowerPoint presentations
- Reading or extracting text from .pptx files
- Combining or splitting slide decks
- Working with templates and layouts
- Adding speaker notes or comments
- Creating pitch decks or presentations

## When NOT to Use

- Marp presentations (use marp-slide)
- PDF files (use pdf skill)
- Real-time collaborative editing
- Simple text documents

## Workflow

### Phase 1: Prepare Presentation

- Determine presentation type and purpose
- Gather content and images
- Plan slide structure

### Phase 2: Create/Edit Slides

- Create new presentation or open existing
- Add slides and content
- Insert images and shapes
- Apply formatting and themes

### Phase 3: Enhance Presentation

- Add speaker notes
- Create animations if needed
- Adjust layouts and templates
- Verify consistency

### Phase 4: Finalize & Export

- Review all slides
- Check formatting
- Export to PDF or other formats
- Save presentation

## Tools & References

- **Related Skills**: marp-slide, frontend-design
- **File Format**: .pptx (Office Open XML)
- **Libraries**: python-pptx, pptx
- **Templates**: Built-in and custom

## Best Practices

- Use consistent templates
- Keep slides uncluttered
- Use high-quality images
- Include speaker notes
- Test on presentation equipment
- Save frequently
- Back up important presentations
